currentUse: Active Catholic worship site (St. Casimir Worship Site) of Saint Andrew Lithuanian Parish, Philadelphia; Sunday 9:00 AM Mass in English. The adjacent former rectory at 324 Wharton St is on the market.
summary: St. Casimir's church at 4th & Wharton Streets in Pennsport, South Philadelphia, is still standing and still in Catholic use: since the parish's suppression on July 1, 2011 it has operated as a worship site of Saint Andrew Lithuanian Parish, and the parish's current website lists a Sunday 9:00 AM Mass in English at the St. Casimir site, with the 11:00 AM Lithuanian-and-English Mass held at St. Andrew church at 19th & Wallace. Longtime pastor Fr. Peter (Petras) Burkauskas became Pastor Emeritus effective August 24, 2021, and Rev. G. Dennis Gill, rector of the Cathedral Basilica of SS. Peter and Paul, was named Parochial Administrator pro tem effective September 10, 2021; both former rectories' phone lines are out of service and parish correspondence now runs through the Cathedral Parish Office. A January 2026 Draugas feature reports active parish life (a roughly 40-student Vincas Krėvė Lithuanian school, visiting Lithuanian priests, a 2024 parish centennial) and says Archbishop Nelson Perez has declined to close the parish, citing that closures scatter about 40% of parishioners; it also reports the St. Andrew rectory sold for about $1.2 million. Meanwhile the former St. Casimir rectory at 324 Wharton St (built 1918, 5,400 sq ft) is actively listed for sale at $799,900 through BHHS Fox & Roach, at 107 days on market as of mid-July 2026. The church building itself has not been sold and no demolition, redevelopment, or formal preservation proceedings were found.

developments.headline: Parish suppressed; church retained as worship site of St. Andrew
developments.detail: The 118-year-old Lithuanian personal parish at 324 Wharton St merged into St. Andrew (Lithuanian) Parish at 19th & Wallace Sts effective July 1, 2011, after 16 years of the parishes being twinned; St. Casimir became a secondary worship site and Sunday Mass continued there. Registered parishioners had fallen from 195 (2000) to 182 (2010), and the building carried roof-repair needs and debt from the school, which had closed in 2007.

sources.title: Immaculate Conception and St. Casimir close

developments.headline: Fr. Burkauskas retires; Cathedral rector becomes administrator, rectories vacated
developments.detail: Rev. Peter M. Burkauskas, the Lithuanian pastor since 1996, became Pastor Emeritus of Saint Andrew Parish effective August 24, 2021; Rev. G. Dennis Gill, Rector of the Cathedral Basilica of SS. Peter and Paul, was appointed Parochial Administrator pro tem of Saint Andrew Parish effective September 10, 2021. The parish website states the former Saint Andrew and Saint Casimir rectory phone lines are no longer in service and all correspondence goes through the Cathedral Parish Office at 1723 Race St.

sources.title: Saint Andrew Lithuanian Parish (official parish website)

developments.headline: Draugas reports parish alive, archbishop against closure; St. Andrew rectory sold for $1.2M
developments.detail: A Draugas feature (Jan 8-9, 2026) reports active life in the merged parish: the Vincas Krėvė Lithuanian school with about 40 students, monthly youth Masses by Fr. Jonas Puodžiūnas, biweekly visits by Fr. Danielius Staniškis, and Fr. Gill (whose uncle founded St. Andrew in 1924) as administrator. It states Archbishop Nelson Perez has refused to close the parish, citing that closures scatter roughly 40% of parishioners, and that the St. Andrew rectory was sold for about $1.2 million with archdiocesan financial guidance. It describes St. Casimir's Pennsport neighborhood as much improved, with property values sharply up.

sources.title: Švento Andriejaus parapijoje Philadelphijoje verda gyvenimas

developments.date: 2026 (listed ca. late March; active as of 2026-07-16)
developments.headline: Former St. Casimir rectory at 324 Wharton St listed for sale at $799,900
developments.detail: The 1918-built, 5,400 sq ft, 12-bedroom former rectory adjacent to the church is on the market (MLS PAPH2601680) at $799,900 through BHHS Fox & Roach-Center City Walnut, showing 107 days on market as of mid-July 2026; it is also marketed on LoopNet as 'Saint Casimir Rectory,' a religious facility. The listing covers the rectory parcel only — the church building remains an active worship site.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record (suppressed to worship-site status July 2011, survives as an open worship site of the merged parish) is confirmed by the archdiocese, CatholicPhilly, and the parish's own website; the suppression date is refined to July 1, 2011. The archive's note that 'Lithuanian-Mass continuity after 2011 is conflicted' is resolved for the present day: the parish website's current schedule shows the St. Casimir worship-site Sunday 9:00 AM Mass is in English, while the Lithuanian (and English) Mass is celebrated at 11:00 AM at St. Andrew church at 19th & Wallace — so Mass continues at St. Casimir, but Lithuanian-language liturgy is now anchored at St. Andrew. One refinement: since September 2021 the merged parish has no resident pastor; it is administered pro tem by the Cathedral rector, which is a real restructuring-exposure signal despite Archbishop Perez's reported no-closure stance (Draugas, Jan 2026).
gaps: Could not verify: (1) whether the January 2026 parish-website Mass schedule remains in force this month — the site is undated and could not be fetched with a dated snapshot; (2) the fate of the former St. Casimir parish school building (school closed 2007; current occupant/ownership not found); (3) any formal archdiocesan pastoral-planning review currently naming St. Andrew/St. Casimir (2026 clerical appointments list contains nothing for this parish); (4) whether the 324 Wharton rectory has gone under agreement since the listing snapshot (107 days on market, still 'Active' at access); (5) the exact date and buyer of the $1.2M St. Andrew rectory sale reported by Draugas — no deed record or second source located; (6) minor address ambiguity: the archdiocese lists the parish at 324 Wharton St (the rectory parcel now for sale) while directories place the church itself at 4th & Wharton / 338 Wharton St. LoopNet's listing page itself returned 403 and was not opened; the rectory listing facts come from the Bright MLS IDX page. Identity of the parish as the Lithuanian St. Casimir (not the Polish St. Casimir elsewhere in PA) is confirmed by the archdiocese and parish site.
